items.title: Wrexham, Middlesbrough, Ipswich in action - EFL on Sky LIVE!
Man United pressure is a privilege says Sesko
items.summary: Benjamin Sesko says he must embrace the pressure that comes with playing for Manchester United if he's going to succeed at Old Trafford.

items.title: PSG's Hakimi to stand trial over rape allegation
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain defender Achraf Hakimi insists he will "allow the truth to come out publicly" after learning he is to face trial for an alleged rape.

items.title: Courtois 'disappointed' with Mourinho's Vini claim
items.summary: Thibaut Courtois was "disappointed" by José Mourinho's claim that Vinícius Júnior's goal celebration led to the star being allegedly racially abused.

items.title: Portugal monitoring unrest before Mexico friendly
items.summary: The Portuguese soccer federation said Tuesday it was monitoring the situation in Mexico amid a surge in violence ahead of its friendly against the Mexican national team in March.

items.title: VI Live: Atlético Madrid slaat meteen na rust toe tegen Club Brugge
items.summary: Internazionale hoopt dinsdagavond de 3-1 uitnederlaag tegen FK Bodø/Glimt weg te poetsen. Verder heeft Club Brugge op bezoek bij Atlético Madrid kans om de achtste finale te halen. Volg de duels in de tussenronde van de Champions League in deze VI Live.

items.title: President Mexico tempert WK-zorgen na dood drugsbaas: 'Er is geen risico'
items.summary: De Mexicaanse president Claudia Sheinbaum heeft benadrukt dat het WK 2026, dat gespeeld wordt in Canada, de Verenigde Staten en Mexico, veilig kan worden georganiseerd, ondanks recente onrust na de dood van een beruchte kartelleider.

items.title: 'Players must not be excluded' - ECB and Hundred teams reaffirm inclusivity commitment
items.summary: The England and Wales Cricket Board and all eight Hundred franchises have vowed no player should be excluded from the competition on grounds of their nationality.

items.title: De wederopstanding van Manchester United: drie dingen die Carrick verandert
items.summary: Sinds Michael Carrick het heeft overgenomen bij Manchester United, heeft de club slechts twee verliespunten geleden in zes wedstrijden. United is slechts drie punten verwijderd van plek drie en heeft goede kansen op Champions League-voetbal. Dankzij deze drie redenen is United gaan winnen onder Carrick.

items.title: Newcastle make seven changes vs Qarabag LIVE!
Williamson 'wouldn't rule out' striking over scheduling
items.summary: England captain Leah Williamson says she would "never rule out" strike action to get players' messaging across to governing bodies about schedule concerns.

items.title: Geschorste Mourinho schittert ook in afwezigheid: 'Real Madrid is bevoordeeld'
items.summary: José Mourinho heeft verstek laten gaan op het persmoment in aanloop naar de return in de tussenronde van de Champions League tegen Real Madrid. Op bezoek bij zijn oude werkgever is de trainer van Benfica geschorst, maar op de persconferentie werd hij gewoon verwacht. Hij schitterde echter door afwezigheid.

items.title: Back at the Bernabéu: Could Mourinho return to coach Real Madrid again?
items.summary: Wednesday's clash isn't just a chance for José Mourinho's Benfica to eliminate Real Madrid from the Champions League. It might also be an audition.

items.title: Prestianni trains at Bernabeu despite match ban over racism allegation
items.summary: Benfica winger Gianluca Prestianni trained alongside his team-mates at Real Madrid's stadium on Wednesday, despite being provisionally banned for Wednesday's Champions League match between the sides.

items.title: Gesprekken met Memphis gaven de doorslag voor Labyad
items.summary: Zakaria Labyad werd dinsdag gepresenteerd als speler van Corinthians en is klaar om te vlammen. De gesprekken met Memphis Depay gaven de doorslag om ook naar Brazilië te komen.

items.title: Williamson: Players could go on strike over scheduling concerns
items.summary: England captain Leah Williamson suggested players would be willing to go on strike if their concerns over player welfare and playing schedules aren't addressed.

items.title: Courtois defends Vinicius Jr: 'He didn't do anything wrong'
items.summary: Real Madrid goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois defended team-mate Vinicius Junior ahead of the second leg of their Champions League knockout play-off against Benfica, saying he did "nothing wrong" while celebrating.

items.title: 'It would be catastrophic' - are Spurs too big to go down?
items.summary: Tottenham's heavy north London derby defeat against Arsenal on Sunday just confirmed what everyone already knew - Igor Tudor's team are in serious trouble. BBC Sport assesses their precarious situation, what relegation might bring and asks if they are too big to go down?

items.title: Arbeloa and Courtois call on Uefa to take stand against racism after Vinícius incident
items.summary: Courtois: ‘This a moment for football to end these things’ Real Madrid meet Benfica in second leg on Wednesday Álvaro Arbeloa and Thibaut Courtois have called on Uefa to take a ­genuine stand against racism and change football following the alleged racist abuse of Vinícius Júnior by Gianluca ­Prestianni during Real Madrid’s Champions League playoff first leg at Benfica last week, with Arbeloa imploring the governing body to go beyond “just slogans” as the two teams prepare to meet again. Courtois also expressed his disappointment with José Mourinho for linking the incident to Vinícius’s cele­bration of the only goal of the game in Lisbon and insisted suggestions that Prestianni’s defence might be that he instead used a homophobic slur would be “just as bad”. Continue reading...
